steps.title: Use the two given values
steps.content: We are told $f(0)=7$ and $f(2)=7$. Since $f$ is linear, the slope is $m=\frac{f(2)-f(0)}{2-0}=\frac{7-7}{2}=0$, so $f$ is constant.

steps.title: Write the function
steps.content: A constant linear function that outputs $7$ for every input is $f(x)=7$.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• A) $f(x)=0$ — gives $f(0)=0$, not $7$. Eliminated.
  • B) $f(x)=2$ — gives $f(0)=2$, not $7$. Eliminated.
  • C) $f(x)=7$ — gives $f(0)=7$ and $f(2)=7$. Matches.
  • D) $f(x)=x+7$ — gives $f(2)=9$, not $7$. Eliminated.
That matches choice C — C is correct.
